description.s: Symbol indexing automatically extracts function, variable, class, and method definitions from code items and stores them as structured metadata in
. This enables symbol-level navigation, transclusion, and code introspection throughout the system.
Every code item in Hobson has its symbols parsed and indexed. When you save a code item, the
fires, parses the code with Acorn via
This happens silently (no re-render, no re-triggering watchers) and is skipped if symbols haven't changed.
field is a flat map keyed by qualified name:
Parent scope name, or null for top-level
Function signature (only present for function-like symbols)
JSDoc description (first line of prose before any @tags)
Function declaration, arrow function, or function expression assigned to a variable
Object literal property assigned a function, or shorthand method

Code item saved
  --> symbol-indexer-watch (item:created / item:updated)
    --> symbol-extractor-lib.extractSymbols(code)
      --> Acorn parses to AST
      --> walkNode() traverses, collecting symbols
      --> attachJSDoc() matches /** */ comments to symbols
    --> Diff against existing _symbols
    --> If changed: silent save to item.content._symbols
The watcher only fires for items whose type chain includes

Markdown links with symbol parameters open the item scrolled to the symbol:
Inside code editors, comment links also support symbols:
